subject: science
title: Grade 3 Science
description: Forces and motion, rocks and minerals, and plant life cycles.

lessons.title: Forces and Motion
lessons.objective: Understand pushes, pulls, and how forces affect movement.
lessons.content:
A FORCE is a push or a pull that can:
  • Make things START moving
  • Make things STOP moving
  • Change DIRECTION of movement
  • Change SPEED (faster or slower)

🏋️ Types of forces:
  PUSH — moves something away from you (kicking a ball)
  PULL — moves something towards you (pulling open a door)
  GRAVITY — pulls everything down toward the ground
  FRICTION — a force that slows things down (rubbing)

FRICTION happens when two surfaces rub together:
  • Rough surfaces = more friction (harder to slide)
  • Smooth surfaces = less friction (easier to slide)

GRAVITY pulls everything toward the Earth — that's why things fall down!

exercises.question: What type of force slows things down when surfaces rub together?
exercises.hint: Think about rubbing your hands together — they get warm and slow down.
exercises.explanation: Friction is the force that slows things down when two surfaces rub together. Rough surfaces create more friction!

exercises.question: Which force pulls objects down toward the ground?
exercises.hint: Why do things fall when you drop them?
exercises.explanation: Gravity is the force that pulls everything toward the Earth. It's why things fall when you drop them!

lessons.title: Rocks and Minerals
lessons.objective: Learn about the three types of rocks and how they form.
lessons.content:
Rocks are made of MINERALS (natural, solid substances).

There are THREE types of rocks:

🌋 IGNEOUS rocks — formed from cooled lava or magma
  Examples: granite, basalt, pumice
  Pumice is so light it can float on water!

🏖️ SEDIMENTARY rocks — formed from layers of sand, mud, and shells pressed together
  Examples: sandstone, limestone, chalk
  Often have FOSSILS inside!

🔥 METAMORPHIC rocks — formed when heat and pressure change existing rocks
  Examples: marble (from limestone), slate (from mudstone)
  Often have beautiful patterns!

The ROCK CYCLE shows how rocks change from one type to another over millions of years.

exercises.question: Which type of rock is formed from cooled lava?
exercises.hint: Think: volcanoes! What comes out of them?
exercises.explanation: Igneous rocks form when hot lava or magma cools down and hardens. 'Ignis' means fire in Latin!

exercises.question: Which type of rock is most likely to contain fossils?
exercises.hint: Fossils form when creatures get buried in layers of sand and mud.
exercises.explanation: Sedimentary rocks form from layers of material pressed together over time, which can trap and preserve fossils!
